clinical_history: 55-yr-old male with chest discomfort 5 days post "coronary artery bypass graft (CABG) surgery".
diagnosis: ECG shows classic findings of acute pericarditis. Note diffuse ST elevations (leads I, II, aVL, aVF, V3-V6) with concomitant PR segment deviations (PR up in lead aVR, down in II, V4-V6). Thus, ST and PR injury current vectors are discordant.  Cardiac enzymes were not elevated.  There was a subtle pericardial friction rub, best heard after reading the ECG, and no evidence of pericardial compression syndrome.
